contentBlocks.original:
सशंखचक्रं सकिरीटकुंडलं सपीतवस्त्रं सरसीरुहेक्षणम् ।
सहारवक्षः स्थलकौस्तुभश्रियं नमामि विष्णुं शिरसा चतुर्भुजम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saśaṃkhacakraṃ sakirīṭakuṃḍalaṃ sapītavastraṃ sarasīruhekṣaṇam .
sahāravakṣaḥ sthalakaustubhaśriyaṃ namāmi viṣṇuṃ śirasā caturbhujam ..
contentBlocks.translation: I salute, prostrate with my head, to that four-armed Lord Vishnu, who is ornamented with the shankh (conch) and chakra (the divine wheel), the crown and ear-drops, yellow silk robes, lotus-eyes, decorative chain-garland, and the precious Kaustubha on the chest.
contentBlocks.meanings: सशंखचक्रं (saśaṃkhacakraṃ) – with conch and discus (sa:with | śaṃkha:conch | cakraṃ:discus); सकिरीटकुंडलं (sakirīṭakuṃḍalaṃ) – with crown and earrings (sa:with | kirīṭa:crown | kuṃḍalaṃ:earrings); सपीतवस्त्रं (sapītavastraṃ) – with yellow garments (sa:with | pīta:yellow | vastraṃ:garments); सरसीरुहेक्षणम् (sarasīruhekṣaṇam) – lotus-eyed (sarasīruha:lotus | īkṣaṇam:eye); सहारवक्षः (sahāravakṣaḥ) – with garlanded chest (sa:with | hāra:garland | vakṣaḥ:chest); स्थलकौस्तुभश्रियं (sthalakaustubhaśriyaṃ) – with Kaustubha jewel glory (sthala:place | kaustubha:Kaustubha jewel | śriyaṃ:glory); नमामि (namāmi) – I salute; विष्णुं (viṣṇuṃ) – Vishnu; शिरसा (śirasā) – with head; चतुर्भुजम् (caturbhujam) – four-armed (catur:four | bhujam:armed);
